replies.author: @glenni83
replies.text: @liljenborg2517 its where you are completly wrong. California are at war with Disney.

replies.author: @liljenborg2517
replies.text:
@glenni83 The people of Cali, yes.  The legislature in Sacramento, okay.
The extreme left wing COURT SYSTEM of Cali, not so much.  They are Fellow Travelers, co-members in the Cult of Woke.  If this were a class action lawsuit of Disneyland employees complaining about being underpaid, maybe.  If it were some environmental group suing Disney to close the Matterhorn down because the endangered red-foot bumble snipe had decided the fake mountain was their favorite home, sure. 
But in a case over DEI standards being "racist" against Christian, white males, no the courts of the 7th Circuit will TOTALLY side with Disney.  (I wouldn't be surprised if they'd try to summarily dismiss the case, because in their book you CAN'T discriminate against "whites").  Because if THIS lawsuit actually sets the precedent that DEI standards are inherently discriminatory, then corporations the Left has been infiltrating and co-opting for YEARS and getting to adopt DEI standards, too will all be at risk.  They CAN'T allow that to happen.

The case would have to either get to a court system outside California, or get to the Supreme Court (in five or eight years) for it to even have a chance.

Wait until you have to go to court, and then trust that your lawyers will somehow be able to... fight the legislation? I'm really at a loss here. The only other option seems to be unmitigated hubris. With people like Iger, that's always a possibility.

author: @DavidByrden1
text:
These Disney policies would make sense and be legal, ONLY in an alternative world.
In that world, every job would attract applicants from all social groups, all equally talented and suitable for the job.
Therefore, a bias in the personnel would not reflect the applicant pool, so it would have to be caused by discrimination in the hiring process.
But we don't live in that alternative world. We live in THIS world.
In our world, certain social groups do certain things better than others. The cause of THAT may be discrimination, yes, but the discrimination  happened OUTSIDE of Disney. It is not Disney's duty to correct it, and in any case, forced hiring of underqualified people does NOT correct that discrimination. it merely creates the VISUAL IMPRESSION that the discrimination never existed. 
It's a cheat. And the shareholders are the people being cheated.

author: @rocistone6570
text: This is why there are buildings full of lawyers in Burbank, LA and NYC as well as Washington DC who work for the Corporate Mouse.  Take a look at the enforcement record of the Feds on complaints like this. I don't want to pee in anyone's beer, but the reality of something like this is as follows. Long, expensive investigation. Lawyers from both sides meet with DOJ people. The defendant (Disney) is given a chance to recant and reform. They commit to evident and documentable changes in practices. If there is any sort of fine or penalty for the offensive conduct, it comes out of Disney's petty cash. The aim of the DOJ is to impose CHANGE not penalty unless the offence(s) are truly egregious in nature. So everyone sharpening their knives, put your blades away. There won't be any fiscally world-shattering amounts of money involved here. I doubt that this even goes to trial. Mostly because it saves money and "face" for everyone involved.

author: @liljenborg2517
text:
This lawsuit might NOT work.
First, racial discrimination in front of the camera is perfectly legal.  If you’re making a tv show about a black family, nobody blinks if all the actors hired are black.  (On the other hand, if you make a show, say a reboot of Lost in Space, about a white family, cobbling a reason to have on of the kids “adopted” to explain race swapping the character is also perfectly okay).
Second, behind the camera, they’ll make the argument, of course we need discriminated groups in the writers room and directors chair because “majority” people are inherently incapable of accurately representing the oppression and discrimination of the black/brown/LGBTQ “lived experience.”
And, in the California courts, these arguments WILL fly.

replies.text: There are plenty of courts in California with counsel that is absolutely against the DEI, and the decline of their state.  Plus, these ARE actually illegal hiring practices.  The point is…..to keep non stop pressure on every business that does this, to reverse this culture of evil, America First Legal has a lot of influential backing and funding, so forcing Disney to fight constant law fare battles will wear them down,  that’s our strategy.  I’m donating to America First Legal, on a regular basis, even if it’s only 20 dollars or even 10 dollars.  Tell everyone you know about this and encourage them to donate.
